mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 12-month windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in 65% or more of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during labor market contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 55% of observations, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output that improves corporate profit margins and drives positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 70% of months where INDPRO increases, or the mechanism fails to generate positive alpha during confirmed expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 3.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in 60% or more of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < -0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in 65% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion that underpins corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 60% or more of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal shows no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.02) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production combined with rising consumer prices creates a stagflationary environment that erodes corporate margins and forces monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of stagflationary trigger months, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market model (Sharpe ratio < 0.0).
